topic: European news on political social media controversies, content removal, and censorship (May 5-12, 2026)

articles.headline_en: Boylan says Dublin Central voters not raising immigration, calls it a non-issue on campaign trail
articles.summary_en: Sinn Féin Dublin Central candidate Janice Boylan stated during her campaign that voters are focused on 'bread and butter issues' rather than immigration. She made these comments amid ongoing election discussions. The article was published on May 6, 2026.
articles.significance: This reflects Sinn Féin's strategy to downplay immigration in Irish elections, potentially amid sensitivities over social media discourse on the topic.

editorial_overview: Limited coverage emerged in the past week on European politicians' social media controversies or censorship. The sole relevant item involves an Irish Sinn Féin candidate dismissing immigration concerns, signaling a pivot to economic priorities. No reports surfaced on deletions, controversial posts, content removals, or platform moderation involving political figures.
